series_description: Effective January 1994 the Board began using computer generated renewal cards. The Board receives quarterly and annual lists from the Division of Computer Services verifying receit of the cards.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by the State Auditor and after report is released

series_description: This record contains the individuals application for licensure as LSW, LISW, LPC, LPCC, SWA, and CA transcripts, reference forms and other documents submitted for review for licensure. The Board has determined that indivduals did not qualify for licensur
retention_statement: Paper: 1 year after appeal time has expired

series_description: This series contians comlaint forms used to file complaints against licensees and non-licensed persons, along with investigative reports and procedures, notes, letters and evidence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area for a period of five years. If complaints are non-chargeable

series_description: Confidential investigation cases and complaints closed by the Board. Cases may be closed because: 1.) They are without merit; 2.) Formal discipline was not initiated but an informal caution was issued; 3.) The licensee or applicant complied with all formal provisions of a disciplinary action.
retention_statement: Paper: Five years after last recorded date of action. If a new case or complaint is received within that five year window older cases may be retained untill the subsequent case is closed.

series_description: Contains continuing education, certificates, copies of certifications, Board approval notices, program brocures, and other continuing education documentation. Board staff reviews to assure that licensees have met their continuing professional education r
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy after reviewed and license is approved for renewal by Board staff.

series_title: Post Program Approval Requests and Notic
series_description: The Board''s rules allow licensees to request approval for continuing professional education that they attend after the completion of the program. These records contain, the post program approval request form, the certificate of attendance and the program
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for at least two years after submission by licensee and after completion of the State Auditors biennial audit is completed the records may

series_description: Paper and licensing system records contain continuing professional education provider applications, which include provider applications, program information, presentor information and evaluation materials.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain as long as a Board approved provider. Destroy one year after provider status ends. ; Electronic: Retain per eLicensing2 system setup and at least five years after expiration

series_description: Paper & electronic records contain continuing professional education applications requesting program approval. Program applications contain approval form, program brochure, presenter resumes and evaluation forms.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year after completion of the program ; Electronic: Retain in licensure system when they become a formal registration with the board. Delete CE related records seven years from the date of the last renewal for which the CE was used to meet CE requirements.

series_description: The Board is required by statute Section 4757.101 of the Revised Code to obtain criminal records checks from BCI&I and FBI for new licensees.
retention_statement: Paper: After one year or sooner if no further use for the reports, they shall be destroyed by securely shredded means. ; Electronic: After one year or sooner if no further use for the reports, they shall be deleted from the Board''s electronic files.

series_title: Online law and rule exam and Continuing Education records
series_description: The board has developed an online laws and rules exam, which each applicant will be required to complete as part of their application. The online exam also includes a continuning education course law and rule review. System records answers to questions and basic information on applicant and pass or fail documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for five years and

series_description: The board receives BCI&I and FBI records checks per section 4757.101 of the Revised Code. These records are invalid one year and a day after receipt.
retention_statement: Paper: One year or after licensure is awarded. Information from reports are noted in the licensing system. ; Electronic: One year after date of receipt for files received by FTP download.
